prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT medium-sized object standing FREE on a small flat patch of dark near-black volcanic rock — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a broken black stone column 2) a black crystal formation 3) a dark rune-carved menhir 4) a small cinder cone venting smoke 5) a scorched dead snag 6) a dark volcanic rock arch 7) a spatter cone of hardened lava 8) a pile of black boulders 9) a lava-rock pillar with glowing seams 10) a cluster of obsidian blades. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
